sentences.benevolence::noun: His benevolence towards the poor was well-known.
sentences.ephemeral::adj: Fame can be an ephemeral thing.
sentences.reciprocal::adj: They had a reciprocal agreement to help each other.
sentences.resilience::noun: Her resilience helped her overcome many challenges.
sentences.autonomy::noun: The region is seeking greater autonomy from the central government.
sentences.ambivalence::noun: She felt a strong sense of ambivalence about the job offer.
sentences.catalyst::noun: Her speech was a catalyst for change in the community.
sentences.nostalgia::noun: He felt a wave of nostalgia for his childhood.
sentences.nuance::noun: The artist carefully nuanced the colors in the painting.
sentences.empirical::adj: The study provided strong empirical evidence for the theory.
sentences.utilitarian::adj: The building had a very utilitarian design, focusing on function over aesthetics.
sentences.itinerant::adj: The itinerant preacher traveled from town to town, spreading his message.
sentences.reconciliation::noun: After years of conflict, they finally achieved reconciliation.
